content_markdown: An organ pipe of length $L$ is open at one end and closed at the other. Which of the following gives the wavelength of the second harmonic standing wave in the pipe?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: For a pipe closed at one end and open at the other, only odd harmonics exist ($n = 1, 3, 5, ...$). The fundamental (first harmonic) has $L = \frac{\lambda}{4}$, so $\lambda_1 = 4L$. The next possible standing wave is the third harmonic (often referred to as the second harmonic in some contexts of this specific boundary condition problem, or the first overtone), where $L = \frac{3\lambda}{4}$. Solving for wavelength gives $\lambda = \frac{4L}{3}$.
